title: rosa gallica var. subtomentella
intro: Rosa gallica var. subtomentella, also known as the Officinal Rose or Rosa Gallica, is a subspecies of the Rosa gallica species, a type of flowering plant native to Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as a natural antiseptic, due to the presence of essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rosa gallica var. subtomentella include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
